sanskrit: ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः । प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ॥
hindiTranslation: भगवान् के विश्वरूपको देखकर अर्जुन बहुत चकित हुए और आश्चर्यके कारण उनका शरीर रोमाञ्चित हो गया ।  वे हाथ जोड़कर विश्वरूप देवको मस्तकसे प्रणाम करके बोले ॥
englishTranslation: Then, overwhelmed with wonder, Arjuna’s hair stood on end. He bowed his head to the divine and spoke with folded hands.
essence: A glimpse of the vast can turn shock into surrender.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna is stunned by the cosmic form. He bows his head, joins his hands, and begins to speak.
simpleInsight: A true encounter with greatness can dissolve pride into reverence.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna has just seen Krishna’s cosmic form filling everything. Overwhelmed, his body reacts before his mind can speak: his hair stands on end, he bows deeply, and only then finds words. The moment marks awe turning into prayer.
modernRelevance: You open a message and realize the truth is bigger than your plan. Before you can argue, your body goes quiet and humility takes over.
translationLiteral: ततः (then) / सः (he) / विस्मय-आविष्टः (filled with wonder) / हृष्ट-रोमा (with hair standing on end) / धनञ्जयः (Arjuna) / प्रणम्य (having bowed) / शिरसा (with the head) / देवम् (to the divine) / कृताञ्जलिः (with folded hands) / अभाषत (spoke)
insight: It shifts the response from analysis to reverent speech when reality exceeds control.
